prompt: Share an untranslatable word or idiom from any language you know about — something that doesn't have a clean English equivalent — and explain what it captures.
response: In Greek, there is the idiom "Να μη λες πολύ, κολέ PS, αγαπώ" which translates to "Don't say much, ok PS, I love you." This idiom doesn't have a clean English equivalent as it captures a unique aspect of Greek communication and culture. Greeks often communicate through tone, gestures, and silence, and this idiom reflects that. The phrase is typically used at the end of a conversation when someone wants to convey their affection without explicitly saying "I love you." The PS is a way of softening the statement and maintaining the lightheartedness of the interaction.
